name: Web Manifests Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof of the changelog-manifest contract (#1057) in a real browser: a per-release manifest of per-module change entries — severity (major/minor/patch), type (added/changed/deprecated/removed/fixed/security), and a migration ref present iff a change is breaking and mechanically applicable. The contract is type-only (manifests/changelog-contract.ts); the manifest reader + integrity pipeline are impl in Frontier UI, so the demo supplies a sample manifest plus its own in-demo reader. A runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live: the manifest declares its release + previous version, entriesFor(module) scopes to the module, bySeverity filters per the vocabulary, breaking changes are the major entries, a migration ref is present iff breaking + applicable, and every entry carries module/severity/type/summary.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof of the changelog-manifest contract (#1057). A per-release manifest of per-module change entries — severity, type, and a migration ref present iff a change is breaking and mechanically applicable.

In-demo reader, not the FUI impl

The contract is type-only (manifests/changelog-contract.ts); the manifest reader + integrity pipeline are impl in Frontier UI. So this demo supplies a sample manifest plus its own in-demo reader to prove the contract is realizable.

Verified contract

The runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live: the manifest declares its release + the version it migrates from, entriesFor(module) scopes to the module, bySeverity filters per the major/minor/patch vocabulary, breaking changes are the major entries, a migration ref is present iff breaking + applicable, and every entry carries module/severity/type/summary.
